search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG12 oral ulcer three weeks oral lump red white patch neck lump
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic biopsy, imaging, dental, oral-surgery, oncology, or ENT referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: NICE provides recognition features and referral context, not diagnosis.
No investigation, biopsy, treatment, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with an oral lesion; recommendation-specific persistence and lesion features apply, while children, acute infection, trauma, immune disease, and known cancer require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, dental, oral medicine, oral surgery, or ENT review.
UAE_applicability: NICE recognition guidance requires UAE dental, oral medicine, pathology, oncology, ENT, and referral-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG12 exact head-and-neck and safety-netting sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NG12 remains current and was last updated on 2026-04-15.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE head-and-neck section supports duration, persistence, oral ulcer, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white patch, associated or negative features, clinician-performed ENT or neck assessment, and clinician concern without asserting oral cancer.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact safety-netting section supports concerns, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without generating biopsy, dental or cancer referral, treatment, or urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

unresolved_source_gaps: Site, size, morphology, colour, ulcer duration, pain, trauma, dental factors, tobacco, alcohol, systemic symptoms, examination, photography, pathology, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
